Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The J is the entry/all-round model of the JX family, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr as the JX with an upgraded anti-static base, finer catch-cup threads and a foldable handle. It uses the classic internal-adjustment dial (30 clicks/rotation, ~25µm steps), which suits pour-over and filter brewing more than precise espresso dialing. The burr delivers a clean, balanced cup with subtle brightness; the coarse step size makes espresso fine-tuning difficult.

The J-Max is the dedicated espresso model of the J series, using a 48mm coated conical burr fixed to the central shaft for grinding stability. Its external super-fine adjustment of 8.8µm per click makes it one of the easiest hand grinders for dialing in espresso, and it ships with a magnetic catch cup. The broad rotation range also covers filter brewing; build is all-metal and premium.

The J-Max S is functionally identical to the J-Max in grind mechanism — same 48mm coated conical espresso burr, 8.8µm external adjustment and magnetic catch cup — with an added foldable handle and improved ergonomic knob for portability and table stability. The very fine step size makes it excellent for espresso dial-in while remaining capable across filter methods. Grind quality is unchanged from the J-Max.

Step Type: External adjustment with rotation indicator, stepped
Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The J-Ultra is the flagship all-rounder/espresso grinder, using a newly designed 48mm espresso-focused coated stainless-steel conical burr fixed to the shaft. Its 8µm-per-click external adjustment plus a clear rotation indicator give the most precise, easiest dial-in of the series, suited to both espresso and pour-over. Lighter (~670g) than the J-Max and includes a magnetic catch cup; the burr produces a clean, sweet, low-fines grind.

The JE is a dedicated espresso grinder using a 47mm coated conical espresso burr, prized for sweet, creamy, thick-bodied shots with prominent mouthfeel. The original JE (2018–2020) used a simpler internal dial with smaller capacity; the current JE-Plus adds a top adjustment dial (12.5µm/click), magnetic catch cup and blind-shaker-friendly design. Note: sources disagree on the original JE's micron-per-click (~25µm vs 12.5µm); confirm which variant this row represents.
Verification Notes: Range OK. micron/step disputed: maker lists JE-Plus 12.5, plain JE unconfirmed - left unknown

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The JX is the original all-round/pour-over grinder of the series, with a 48mm stainless-steel conical burr (shared with the JX-Pro) that yields a balanced, slightly bright cup. Its internal-adjustment dial with 30 clicks/rotation and ~25µm steps suits filter brewing (V60, AeroPress, French press) but is too coarse-stepped for precise espresso. Solidly built all-metal body; the J model is essentially its modernized successor.

The JX S shares the JX's grind mechanism exactly — same 48mm conical burr, internal adjustment, 30 clicks/rotation and ~25µm steps — adding a foldable handle for storage, better table stability and an improved knob for smaller hands. It remains a pour-over/all-round grinder; grind quality and brewing behavior are identical to the JX.

The JX-Pro is the versatile espresso-and-filter grinder, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr as the JX but with a top-mounted adjustment dial offering finer 12.5µm steps and the burr/shaft fixed together for consistency. The finer adjustment makes espresso dial-in genuinely workable while the wide range covers all filter methods, making it 1Zpresso's most recommended all-rounder. Uses a threaded catch cup (no magnet) and is well-built all-metal.

The JX-Pro S is mechanically identical to the JX-Pro — same 48mm conical burr, top adjustment, 40 clicks/rotation and 12.5µm steps — with an added foldable handle, improved grip knob and the storage/stability benefits that brings. It remains a strong espresso-plus-filter all-rounder; grind quality and dial-in behavior match the JX-Pro exactly.

A premium all-rounder built around a 48mm coated stainless-steel conical burr. The grind range runs from very fine to very coarse (~0–1400µm), covering espresso through French press and cold brew. The external numbered dial allows fast, repeatable adjustment, and the coated burr produces a clean, consistent particle distribution well suited to espresso dial-in. Heavier and aimed at serious home use rather than ultralight travel.

The K-series espresso/all-round model with a 48mm stainless-steel conical burr and a magnetic catch cup (versus the K-Pro's threaded cup). Grind range ~0–1330µm spans espresso to coarser filter and immersion brews. The fine external stepping gives precise espresso dial-in, and the burr geometry yields a consistent grind supporting clean extractions across pour-over and espresso. Solidly built for home use.

The value entry point of the K-series, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr set as its siblings but with a threaded catch cup rather than magnetic, and a maximum dose around 30–35g. External adjustment makes it fast and easy to dial in across espresso through filter, and the 48mm burr delivers good consistency at both fine and coarse grinds. Ergonomic wood knob; moderate grind speed.

1Zpresso's flagship K-series all-rounder, built around a 48mm heptagonal stainless-steel conical burr with tight assembly tolerancing, a foldable handle and carrying case. The finer 20-micron stepping and 100-click rotation give more granular dial-in than the rest of the K-series, suiting it equally to espresso and pour-over. The heptagonal burr produces very uniform particle distribution for clean, balanced extractions, and the external numbered adjustment makes brew-to-brew repeatability easy.
Verification Notes: Range matches source (note: 760 max is low vs K-Max/K-Pro 1400)

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: An ultralight travel/beginner pour-over grinder (~365g) small enough to fit inside an AeroPress plunger. It uses a 38mm stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing, triaxial shaft for stability. The coarser 25-micron internal stepping is geared toward filter/pour-over rather than espresso precision, but the heptagonal burr still delivers consistent grounds for its size and price class. Tool-free disassembly preserves calibration during cleaning.

The current international Q2 configuration, fitted with a 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing shaft. The heptagonal geometry grinds slightly slower than the older pentagonal burr but produces a more uniform particle distribution with fewer fines, improving cup clarity for pour-over and AeroPress. Compact (~475g) travel grinder; the coarse internal stepping makes it best for filter rather than fine espresso.

The original Q2 burr geometry — a 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel pentagonal conical burr. The pentagonal design cuts faster than the heptagonal version but produces slightly more fines, giving a marginally less uniform grind that adds body but can reduce clarity, especially in lighter pour-overs. Same compact dual-bearing travel chassis as the heptagonal Q2; best for medium-fine to medium-coarse filter brewing.

A refined Q2 with the upgraded 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing, triaxial shaft (~465g). The heptagonal burr gives more uniform grounds and fewer fines than the original pentagonal Q2 for cleaner filter and AeroPress cups. As with the rest of the Q line, the 25-micron internal stepping targets pour-over/travel use rather than precise espresso.

Uses 40mm heptagonal stainless-steel conical burrs in a high-grade aluminum-alloy body (~610g). The external adjustment ring is the headline feature — far quicker and more intuitive than older internal designs — and the burrs show better alignment than 1Zpresso's Q-series, yielding a cleaner, more consistent grind. A versatile all-rounder that excels at filter/pour-over while still being fine enough to dial in espresso.

Mechanically identical to the X-Pro — same 40mm heptagonal steel conical burrs and 60-click/12.5-micron external adjustment. The 'S' denotes the updated foldable handle, making it more compact and travel-friendly; burr material and adjustment performance are unchanged. Best suited to filter/pour-over with capable espresso fine-tuning.

Uses 40mm heptagonal burrs (an upgraded X-series geometry) in an aluminum-alloy body with a removable external adjustment dial for easy zero calibration. The 12.5-micron stepped increments sit in a versatile sweet spot — fine enough for meaningful espresso adjustments yet coarse enough that pour-over settings don't require excessive clicking. Marketed as the most versatile X-series model across drip, AeroPress and espresso.

Uses the specialized 48mm hexagonal stainless-steel 'ZP6 burr' engineered for unimodal particle distribution and maximum cup clarity in pour-over — a single-purpose filter/pour-over grinder, not for espresso (range 240–1050µm). It is the grinder Carlos Medina used to win the 2023 World Brewers Cup. The design centers on clean, consistent grinds and easy disassembly; the original ZP6's exact click count is not reliably documented.

Uses the same specialized 48mm hexagonal stainless-steel 'ZP6 burr' optimized for unimodal grind distribution and exceptional pour-over clarity, housed in the K-Pro body (~750g). It is the relaunched, refined version of the ZP6 — same dedicated filter/pour-over mission but with a finer, more intuitive top dial (90 clicks, shared with the K-series award-winning ring) for precise dialing of clarity and sweetness. A purpose-built pour-over grinder, not for espresso (range 240–1050µm).

60mm conical-burr single-dose grinder with stepless adjustment displayed as rotations.number.tick (e.g. 1+3.2). All-rounder built for both espresso and filter, with a heavy aluminium body and built-in scale-aware single-dosing workflow. Adjustment is effectively stepless so micron-per-step is not fixed; the very wide 0-1400 range covers Turkish through cold brew.

Commercial-style on-demand grinder using large flat burrs (Anfim uses 75mm-class flat burrs on its shop-grade models). Primarily an espresso/all-round shop grinder with stepped worm-gear collar adjustment. Built with a heavy-duty motor and metal body for high throughput.

Dedicated commercial espresso grinder with flat burrs, designed for a narrow espresso-focused range (170-510 microns). Stepped adjustment via numbered collar with sub-steps; the tight range reflects its single-purpose espresso role. Heavy metal construction for cafe use.

Compact electric home grinder with conical burrs and click-stepped adjustment from 0 to 23 clicks. Positioned as an all-round home grinder though best suited to medium and coarser filter brews; the manufacturer warns against the finest settings (0-6) which can damage the grinder. Lightweight consumer build rather than a precision espresso unit.

Entry-level all-rounder using Baratza 38mm M2 conical steel burrs. 40-step external stepped adjustment ring; intended for filter/drip, pour over, French press and AeroPress rather than consistent espresso. Reliable, repairable build that became the standard beginner home grinder.

Espresso-capable version of the Encore using 40mm M2 conical burrs with a recalibrated gearbox so more of the 40 steps fall in the espresso range. All-rounder that can pull home espresso plus do filter brews on the same robust repairable Encore chassis. Steps 1-20 weighted toward espresso, 21-40 toward filter and coarser.

Updated Pro version of the Encore ESP with 40mm conical burrs and a finer, higher-resolution stepped scale for more espresso precision. All-round home grinder skewing toward espresso plus filter brewing. Maintains Baratza's repairable design and adds threadless burr access and improved adjustment granularity.

Commercial-grade grinder with 54mm flat steel burrs (AP = All Purpose). Dual macro/micro stepped adjustment (numbered macro ring 1-10 plus lettered micro steps A-Z) for fine control across filter and espresso. Ships with steel flat burrs tuned for all-purpose brewing; metal build with digital display and timed dosing.

Commercial-grade grinder essentially identical to the Forté AP but BG (Brew Grinder) ships with 54mm ceramic flat burrs tuned toward filter/brewed coffee. Same dual macro/micro stepped adjustment and digital dosing interface. Heavy metal construction intended for prosumer and light commercial use.

Older entry-level conical-burr home grinder with 40 external stepped settings. All-round home use leaning toward filter and drip brews; not a true espresso grinder. Predecessor design to the Encore family with a plastic consumer body.

Upgraded version of the Maestro with the same 40-step external stepped conical-burr adjustment and identical 200-1010 micron range. All-round home grinder for filter/drip; the Plus added a timer and higher-torque motor over the base Maestro. Discontinued legacy model preceding the Encore.

Higher-precision sibling of the Maestro/Virtuoso line using 40mm conical burrs plus a micro-adjust collar, giving 40 macro steps each subdivided by lettered micro steps. Designed as an all-rounder with enough fine resolution to attempt espresso as well as filter. Discontinued legacy model that introduced micro-stepping to Baratza's consumer range.

Espresso-focused single-dose grinder with 40mm conical burrs in an upper-burr-rotating design that grinds straight down for very low retention and fast output. 30 outer macro steps plus a 9-position inner micro ring for fine espresso control. Tall, fast and somewhat loud; excellent for espresso and capable of filter at coarser settings.

Same espresso-focused Sette platform as the 270 (40mm conical burrs, low-retention straight-down grinding) with the addition of a built-in single-weight grind-by-weight scale. 30 macro plus 9 micro stepped settings. Grinds by target weight for a single dose; otherwise identical grind behavior to the 270.

Top Sette model with intelligent grind-by-weight (Wi) that auto-adjusts grind time based on measured output for repeatable doses. Same 40mm conical-burr low-retention straight-down design and 30 macro plus 9 micro stepped adjustment. Espresso-focused single-doser also usable for filter at coarser settings.

Budget version of the Sette platform with 40mm conical burrs and the same fast, low-retention straight-down grind path but only the 30 macro stepped settings (no fine micro-adjust ring). Espresso-capable single-doser aimed at value buyers; the coarser resolution makes dialing espresso less precise than the 270 series.

Rebadged Baratza Maestro-class conical-burr home grinder sold under the Starbucks Barista name. 40-step external stepped adjustment with a 200-1010 micron range identical to the Maestro. All-round home grinder for drip/filter; legacy consumer model.

Prosumer grinder with 54mm ceramic flat burrs and dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment (numbered macro 1-10 plus lettered micro steps). True all-rounder capable of both espresso and filter; ceramic burrs run cool and stay sharp. Digital display, programmable timed dosing and metal-and-plastic build; can be upgraded to steel flat burrs.

Grind-by-weight (W) version of the Vario with 54mm ceramic flat burrs and the same dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment. Adds an integrated scale to dose by weight rather than time. Prosumer all-rounder for espresso and filter; cool-running ceramic burrs and metal build.

Updated grind-by-weight Vario+ generation with 54mm steel flat burrs (upgraded from ceramic), improved gearbox and a redesigned single-dose-friendly chassis. Dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment plus an integrated scale for dosing by weight. Prosumer all-rounder excelling at both espresso and filter.

Refreshed Vario (2023 generation) with 54mm steel flat burrs, a sturdier metal body, improved gearbox and lower retention than the original Vario. Dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment for fine control. Prosumer all-rounder strong at espresso and filter; timed dosing (non-weight version).

Mid-range home grinder using Baratza 40mm M3 conical steel burrs, a step up from the Encore with a higher-torque motor and more uniform grind. 40-step external stepped adjustment. All-rounder best for filter, pour over and French press; can reach espresso-ish fineness but is not a dedicated espresso grinder.

Upgraded Virtuoso with 40mm conical steel burrs, a digital countdown timer and a slightly wider range (200-1200 microns) than the original Virtuoso. 40-step external stepped adjustment. Premium all-round home grinder for filter and drip with a metal-trimmed build; finest settings approach but are not ideal for true espresso.

Electric grinder with 29mm stainless steel flat/conical burrs and 40 click-stop settings from coarse to fine. Low-speed DC motor around 415 rpm reduces heat, with a 240g bean hopper and 120g grounds container. Marketed as an all-round home/office grinder for filter, brew and lighter espresso; consistency is good for the price but not espresso-specialist level.

Manual hand grinder using a conical burr (commonly stainless steel) with an external numbered click-adjustment dial. Compact and portable, aimed at all-round home brewing across espresso to French press. Detailed manufacturer step counts and micron-per-step data are not reliably published for this generic-branded model.

Electric grinder with a 420 stainless steel conical burr and 51 stepped coarseness settings selected via a touch/button panel with memory function. Anti-static, direct-drop chute design reduces spill and retention. Marketed as an affordable all-round grinder for espresso through French press; build and consistency are entry-level.

High-end commercial/prosumer grinder with 63mm vertical flat special-steel burrs and stepless adjustment guided by a lens display indicating particle size. Powerful 660W motor at 1400 rpm, 3-8 g/s throughput, double ventilation cooling and a vibration chute giving very low retention (about 0.1-0.4g). All-round capable for espresso and filter, built for high-volume precision dial-in.

Electric grinder with conical stainless steel burrs and 12 stepped settings shown as dots from Fine to Coarse. Low-speed motor to limit heat, push-to-grind operation with a friction-clutch borosilicate glass catch cup that reduces static. Intended for all-round home filter, French press and pour-over; not fine enough for true espresso. Build is mostly plastic, entry-level consistency.

Manual hand grinder whose setting is expressed in three parts (rotations, number, tick) indicating a multi-rotation external stepped adjustment with fine increments. Uses a conical burr suited to a wide range from espresso to cold brew. Detailed manufacturer burr-size and micron-per-step specs are not reliably published for this model.

Electric grinder (Sage BCG600) with 40mm stainless steel conical burrs and 60 stepped grind settings via an external dial. Electronic timed dosing in 0.2s increments, hands-free portafilter cradle and direct-grind chute. Aimed at all-round home use from espresso to French press; capable but the 60-step resolution limits very fine espresso dial-in.

Electric grinder (Sage BCG820) with stainless steel conical burrs and 60 external stepped settings, plus a secondary adjustable upper burr for extending the range. Precision digital dosing in 0.2s increments, 450g hopper, LCD control and 50-58mm portafilter cradles. All-round grinder handling espresso through French press; good consistency for the price though not single-dose optimized.

Ultralight (about 295g) portable manual grinder (the Krinder) with Italian hardened/tempered conical steel burrs and a detachable handle. Adjustment is a coarse stepped rotary dial scaled 1-8, with large jumps between steps that make precise espresso dial-in difficult. Best for travel and all-round filter/French press brewing rather than espresso; 30g bean capacity.

Electric grinder with commercial-grade conical steel burrs and 16 stepped settings (4 each across extra fine, fine, regular, coarse). Slow gear-reduction motor under 450 rpm minimizes heat, static and noise. All-round home grinder good for drip, pour-over and French press and capable of fine grinds, though step resolution limits true espresso precision.

Updated Infinity with commercial-grade conical steel burrs and the same 16 stepped settings across extra fine to coarse. Slow sub-450 rpm gear-reduction motor reduces heat and static, with a removable upper burr for cleaning and a more refined housing. All-round home grinder for drip, pour-over and French press; capable of fine grinds but step count limits espresso dial-in.

Premium manual grinder with 39mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burrs and an internal stepped click adjustment of roughly 30 microns per click. Excellent build with glass catch jars and very high grind uniformity across espresso to French press. The benchmark all-round hand grinder, though the standard axle is a touch coarse-stepped for espresso (addressed by Red Clix).

Same C40 MK4 with 39mm Nitro Blade conical burrs but fitted with the Red Clix finer-stepped axle upgrade, roughly halving each step to about 15 microns per click and doubling the number of adjustment steps. This gives far finer espresso dial-in resolution while retaining the same overall range. Excellent all-round and espresso-capable manual grinder.

Larger flagship manual grinder with a 60mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burr (the biggest in the Comandante range) for 2-3x faster grinding. Uses the GX50 Gold Clix click system; standard configuration steps are about 21 microns per click. Premium build with Big Joe handle (about 1000g); excellent all-round and espresso-capable performance with higher throughput than the C40.

C60 Baracuda with the finer-stepped Gold Clix (GX50) axle upgrade, roughly doubling the number of click stops for finer dial-in while keeping the same range. Uses the same 60mm Nitro Blade conical burr. High-throughput, espresso-capable premium manual grinder.

Rugged travel-oriented version of the C40 MK4 with the same 39mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burrs and internal stepped click adjustment of roughly 30 microns per click. Built with a more durable polymer body and protective sleeve for portability. All-round manual grinder spanning espresso to French press with the same high uniformity as the C40.

Travel-focused X25 with the same 39mm Nitro Blade conical burrs fitted with the Red Clix finer-stepped axle upgrade, roughly halving each step to about 15 microns per click for precise espresso dial-in. Durable portable body with the same grind quality as the C40 MK4. Excellent all-round and espresso-capable manual grinder for travel.

This commercial-style on-demand espresso grinder uses 58mm flat stainless steel burrs driven by a 210W direct-drive motor at around 1340 RPM. Build is heavy cast metal (about 6kg) with a portafilter push-to-grind cradle for hands-free dosing. Primarily an espresso grinder, with stepless micrometric adjustment giving fine control in the espresso-to-filter range. Solid low-retention single-dosing performance for home and light commercial use.

The K3 Touch shares the 58mm flat stainless steel burr set and 210W motor of the K3 line but swaps the push cradle for a touchscreen interface with timed/programmable dosing. Stepless micrometric grind adjustment provides very fine resolution across espresso and filter ranges. Heavy metal-bodied build aimed at espresso-focused home and prosumer users. Direct-drive single-dose grinding with relatively low retention.

The K3 Touch Advanced is the top K3 variant with 58mm flat stainless steel burrs, 210W direct-drive motor and an enhanced touchscreen with more precise programmable dosing and grind memory. Stepless micrometric collar allows continuous fine adjustment, well suited to dialing in espresso. Robust all-metal construction for prosumer espresso use, designed for single-dosing with low retention.

A compact Japanese electric grinder using a conical burr set with a straight-down grind path to minimize retention and keep grounds fresh. Runs a 150W motor at roughly 420 RPM with about 240g hopper capacity, weighing around 2.5kg. Infinitely adjustable from very fine to coarse, positioned as an all-rounder that can also pull espresso. Marketed to specialty drinkers wanting a uniform grind with minimal fines and easy washable parts.

A programmable automatic burr mill with flat-style burrs and an 18-position stepped grind dial spanning fine to coarse, plus a cup-count timer. Plastic-bodied consumer machine with an integrated bean hopper and grounds chamber. Best suited to drip, pour over and French press; the finest setting is too coarse for true espresso. Grind consistency is modest, typical of an entry-level home burr grinder.

The Supreme Grind Automatic Burr Mill uses flat burrs with an 18-step grind selector and a separate 4-to-18 cup dose dial. Inexpensive plastic-bodied consumer grinder with a clear bean hopper and removable grounds container. Aimed at drip, pour over and French press rather than espresso, as even its finest setting stays in the medium-fine range. Consistency and fines control are basic, appropriate for casual home use.

An entry-level electric burr grinder with flat burrs and a stepped dial offering settings from fine to coarse with a cup-quantity selector. Lightweight plastic body with bean hopper and removable grounds bin. Intended as an all-round home grinder for drip, French press and similar brew methods; the finest setting is not fine enough for proper espresso. Grind uniformity is modest, typical for the price class.

A step up from the KG79 with a larger bean hopper and grounds capacity, using the same flat-burr design and a stepped fine-to-coarse dial plus cup-count selector. Plastic-bodied consumer machine for everyday home use. Best for drip, pour over and French press rather than espresso, since even the finest setting remains medium-fine. Grind consistency is basic and consumer-grade.

A compact USB-rechargeable portable electric grinder using a 38mm multi-flute (10-blade) conical burr, with a roughly 50g hopper and travel carrying case. The grind dial offers fine stepless-style adjustment described as up to 80 levels from espresso to coarse. Designed as a travel and all-round grinder powered from any 5V/2A USB source. Conical burr gives reasonable consistency for its portable category.

A premium Swiss manual grinder using a 32mm hardened stainless steel (62 HRC) conical burr in a Rotating Ring Burr (RRB) system where the outer ring rotates around a stationary inner cone. Anodized aluminium body (about 705g) with a Tritan grounds cup and an Antistatic-Jig that compresses grounds to cut static and clumping. External stepped adjustment offers 88 precise clicks. A true all-rounder handling everything from espresso to French press with high uniformity and low retention.
Verification Notes: micron/step 20 per Etzinger official (88 steps x 0.02mm)

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The Trim is the slimmer etz-I variant with a 48mm midsection and lighter weight (about 665g) but the same 32mm hardened stainless steel conical RRB burr and Antistatic-Jig as the Regular. Anodized aluminium build with Tritan grounds cup and 88-click external stepped adjustment. Burr geometry and grind range match the Regular, so it is an all-round manual grinder from espresso to filter. Chosen for a more compact, ergonomic hold while keeping the same grind performance.
Verification Notes: micron/step 20 per Etzinger official (88 steps x 0.02mm)

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The etz-U is the retention-free, no-Antistatic-Jig sibling of the etz-I, aimed at users comfortable with RDT/WDT techniques. It uses the same 32mm hardened stainless steel conical RRB burr geometry and anodized aluminium construction with 88-click external stepped adjustment. Cones share geometry with the etz-I but use different threads and are not interchangeable. A high-performance all-round manual grinder for espresso through filter with very low retention.
Verification Notes: micron/step 20 per Etzinger official (88 steps x 0.02mm)

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A single-dose-friendly electric grinder built around 60mm flat hardened-steel burrs in a metal body, designed primarily for espresso but capable across the full range. Uses Eureka stepless micrometric worm-gear adjustment for very fine, repeatable dialing. Relatively quiet for a 60mm grinder with low retention and clump-crushing technology. A prosumer all-rounder leaning espresso, suited to home and light commercial use.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The Atom 75 uses larger 75mm flat burrs in a robust metal chassis for faster, cooler grinding and excellent espresso consistency, while still covering filter grinds. Stepless micrometric adjustment gives continuous fine control with high repeatability. Aimed at demanding home users and cafes, it offers low retention and quiet operation for its burr size. A flagship Atom espresso-focused all-rounder.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A classic shop-style doser grinder using 50mm flat burrs in a tall metal-and-plastic body with a traditional dosing chamber and lever. Stepped grind collar with a numbered fine-to-coarse range. The relatively coarse maximum and fine minimum suit espresso and traditional cafe service rather than coarse brew methods. A simpler, older-design Eureka aimed at espresso and retail (drogheria) use.

A compact home grinder using 50mm flat hardened-steel burrs in the popular Mignon chassis, with stepless micrometric adjustment measured in rotations plus fractional numbers. Primarily an espresso grinder but covers filter and French press across its wide range. Low retention with quiet, direct-drive operation and a small footprint. A strong value all-rounder leaning espresso for home use.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A filter-oriented Mignon variant fitted with larger 65mm flat burrs tuned for clarity in pour over and other brewed methods, reflected in its narrower, finer maximum range. Stepless micrometric adjustment provides precise dialing across its espresso-to-filter span. Compact metal-bodied home grinder with low retention and quiet operation. Best suited to filter and all-round brewing with espresso capability.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The Oro XL shares the 65mm flat-burr filter-tuned setup of the Oro in a slightly enhanced package with larger hopper and dosing accommodation. Stepless micrometric adjustment gives fine continuous control over grind size. Compact metal-bodied home grinder optimized for filter and pour over with espresso capability. Low retention and quiet operation typical of the Mignon line.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: An espresso-focused Mignon with 50mm flat hardened-steel burrs and added sound-dampening for notably quiet operation. Stepless micrometric adjustment is read in rotations plus numbers for very fine repeatable dialing. Compact metal body with low retention and direct-drive motor, well suited to home espresso while still covering filter and French press. A popular quiet all-rounder leaning espresso.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A specialty-oriented Mignon using 55mm flat hardened-steel burrs with stepless micrometric adjustment read in rotations plus numbers, often paired with a touch display and timed dosing. Compact, quiet metal-bodied home grinder leaning espresso but covering the full filter range. Low retention and excellent dialing precision for the price. One of the most popular prosumer home espresso all-rounders.
Verification Notes: Stepless micrometric (Eureka) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A countertop electric grinder with professional-grade 64mm flat burrs built purely for filter/brewed coffee (pour over, drip, batch, French press) and not espresso. Uses 11 marked settings with 31 detented click steps via a front dial, with single-dose loading and a magnetic catch cup. Stainless steel flat burrs deliver an even, brew-focused particle distribution; aftermarket SSP 64mm burr upgrades are available. Solid metal build aimed at the premium home-brewing market.

An electric flat-burr brew grinder using upgraded 64mm stainless steel burrs designed in-house by Fellow, extending the fine range down toward 200-300 microns versus the Gen 1. Retains the 11-setting front dial with 31 click steps, single-dose workflow, anti-static technology and auto-stop. Built for filter methods (pour over, drip, batch, French press) rather than espresso, with improved particle uniformity. Heavy metal construction targeting serious home brewers.

A 40mm conical-burr electric grinder (Fellow six-blade C6-40 Burly Burrs) aimed at all-round home brewing from espresso through filter to cold brew. Offers 41 stepped grind settings via an external collar, single-dose hopper, anti-static technology and a quiet motor. The conical steel burrs give it espresso capability the filter-only Ode lacks, making it Fellow versatile budget-friendly all-rounder. Mostly plastic body with metal accents.

A premium manual hand grinder fitted with large 58mm flat steel M340 burrs (the same alloy used in Fiorenzato commercial grinders), unusually big for a hand grinder. The B-Modal (multipurpose) burr set is versatile across espresso and filter, giving a balanced cup with sweetness, body and clarity. Adjustment is micro-stepped and read in two parts (Number.Step), with a vertical burr orientation, aluminium and food-grade plastic body and roughly 60g capacity. Built for enthusiasts wanting one grinder for both espresso and brew.

The same 58mm flat-steel-burr manual hand grinder as the B-Modal but fitted with M-Modal (filter-specialist) burrs whose geometry produces more uniform particles for maximum separation and cup clarity. Best suited to pour over and other filter methods rather than espresso. Micro-stepped adjustment read in two parts (Number.Step), commercial-grade M340 burr material, aluminium and food-grade plastic construction with around 60g capacity. Aimed at filter-focused enthusiasts chasing flavour clarity.

A versatile single-dose electric grinder (co-developed with Fiorenzato, mechanically near-identical to its counterpart) using 64mm titanium-coated flat steel burrs. A stepped, clearly-labelled adjustment collar marked in numbered dots covers espresso, moka and filter ranges, with each step clicking for easy return to a setting. Offers two programmable timed doses plus continuous grinding and low retention. Sturdy build aimed at home and light commercial use across all brew methods.
Verification Notes: OK - source brands it Fiorenzato (Sanremo); no official micron/step

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: An evolution of the Allground adding a touchscreen/digital interface (the Sense control system) while keeping the same 64mm titanium-coated flat steel burrs and grind range. Uses the same numbered-dot stepped collar covering espresso through filter, with programmable timed dosing and continuous grind. Single-dose oriented with low retention and a robust metal build. Targeted at home and prosumer users wanting all-method versatility with smarter dosing controls.
Verification Notes: OK - source brands it Fiorenzato (Sanremo); no official micron/step

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A premium manual hand grinder built around Swiss-made 47mm Etzinger hardened tool-steel conical burrs, designed to span espresso to French press. Adjustment is via a Lift, Turn and Lock crown offering 72 stepped clicks of roughly 0.02mm (20 microns) each for repeatable settings. Features a full-wrap silicone grip and a robust drive system for stable, repeatable grinding. Aimed at espresso-focused users wanting precise, repeatable manual grinding.

A heavy Japanese-made single-speed electric grinder (Mirukko line) famous for its vertical ghost-teeth conical burrs, with the burr material switched from cast iron to stainless in the mid-2010s. The numbered 1-10 dial after calibration sets filter/brew grinds; espresso is only achievable with the optional flat burr set installed. The fast motor and tank-like body stay planted without vibration. Prized by enthusiasts for durability and distinctive grind character.

A compact 2-in-1 grinder that works as both an electric and a manual hand grinder, using a 47/32mm conical steel burr held by a 10mm shaft on twin precision bearings. Adjustment uses a stepped collar read as rotation-and-number (rotations from zero), offering around 240 grind settings spanning Turkish and espresso through to cold brew. Single-dose with near-zero retention and a high-speed AC motor geared down to about 360 rpm. An SCA award-winning, travel-friendly all-rounder.

An ergonomic manual hand grinder with a side-mounted crank and 40mm conical ceramic burrs, designed for filter and brewed methods rather than true espresso. Offers 8 main numbered stepped settings with half-steps in between for 15 grind sizes, selected via an external dial. The ceramic burr is stabilised at three points on a stainless steel shaft to reduce wobble and improve consistency. Grind quality is even at medium settings but less consistent on coarse; aimed at home brewers wanting repeatable, easy adjustment.

A compact manual hand grinder with conical ceramic burrs that stay sharp and transfer little heat, housed in a methacrylate-resin body with a stainless-steel shaft and handle, holding about 24g. Grind size is set by a burr-mounted adjustment nut that clicks: tighten fully, then back off counting clicks. Low retention thanks to the drop-through conical design, but coarse-end consistency is limited by burr wobble; aimed at travel and entry-level filter/espresso brewing.

A slim, very portable manual grinder using conical ceramic burrs in a plastic (methacrylate-resin) body with a stainless shaft, capacity around 24g. Grind setting is adjusted via an internal stepped click nut beneath the burrs, with no printed scale. The base model suffers from upper-burr wobble that hurts grind uniformity, especially at coarser settings; best suited to budget single-cup filter and travel use.

An upgraded slim manual grinder keeping the conical ceramic burrs but adding a cast-aluminium handle, stainless-steel body and a tighter burr mount that reduces the wobble of the base Slim for noticeably better grind consistency. Capacity is about 24g and adjustment is a stepped click wheel/nut under the burrs. The sturdier build and improved alignment make it a strong portable filter grinder; ceramic burrs keep grinding cool and durable.

A classic tabletop manual grinder with conical ceramic burrs, a glass catch jar and a hand crank, holding roughly 100g. Grind size is set steplessly via an internal nut beneath the burrs, requiring partial disassembly to change. The original is hampered by an unstabilised upper burr that allows side play, causing inconsistent and bimodal grinds at coarser settings; ceramic burrs run cool but the model is now discontinued in favour of the Plus and Pro.

A revised version of the original Skerton with conical ceramic burrs, a glass jar and a roughly 100g capacity, adding a partial upper-burr stabiliser and a hexagonal hopper to curb beans flying out. Grind adjustment remains a stepless internal nut that needs the burr assembly partly dismantled to change. Consistency improved over the original but the coarse range is still criticised, leading Hario to release the Pro; ceramic burrs keep heat low for filter and French press brewing.

The most refined Skerton, using conical ceramic burrs with a fully stabilised upper burr and spring-loaded lower burr for markedly better alignment and coarse-grind consistency. Crucially it moves to an external stepped dial beneath the burrs so grind size can be changed without disassembly, plus a strengthened crank and plastic hopper lid. With a glass jar and around 100g capacity, it is a budget-friendly cool-running grinder well suited to pour-over, French press and cold brew.

An ultra-compact manual travel grinder with conical ceramic burrs and a largely methacrylate-resin body, stainless shaft and roughly 24g capacity. Grind size is set by an unmarked bottom adjustment nut (clockwise finer, anticlockwise coarser) with no printed scale, so settings are dialled by feel and testing. It can be converted to electric via Hario Electro-Solo/motor attachment; ceramic burrs grind cool and the slim profile prioritises portability over coarse-grind precision.

A compact electric burr grinder using a stainless-steel conical burr, with a footprint about the size of A5 paper and a hopper/catch capacity near 100g. It offers 39 stepped fineness levels covering coffee press through espresso, with an angled outlet to reduce clogging and a washable outer burr for easy cleaning. The stainless conical burr produces an even particle size; aimed at home brewers wanting consistent push-button grinding across many brew methods.

An electric V60 grinder with a stainless-steel conical burr and a compact body, providing 39 stepped grind levels selected on a dial for brew methods from press to espresso. Capacity is around 100g with an angled, anti-clog outlet and a removable washable outer burr. The conical burr grinds evenly with minimal fines; this E variant is the European/standard full-size model designed for everyday home filter and drip brewing.

A premium unibody manual grinder machined from a single block of 6000-series aluminium, weighing about 575g with roughly 25g capacity and a 38mm Italian-made conical steel burr set. It uses a stepless adjustment knob (with a marked solid-dot reference among the dots to count rotations) for fine control, and ships with interchangeable conventional (filter-oriented) and contemporary (espresso-oriented) burrs. The unibody build maximises burr alignment for excellent consistency, and a universal interface lets it be driven by a power tool.

A high-end, heavy (about 1.785kg) manual grinder built on a unibody machined chassis fitted with 71mm Mazzer conical burrs (as used in the Mazzer Robur), aimed primarily at espresso. It uses a stepless adjustment with 60 reference marks and a multi-gear reduction crank that makes turning easy while taking around 55 seconds for 18g. A magnetic catch cup fits a 58mm basket with near-zero retention, and grounds come out fluffy and clump-free, giving cafe-grade espresso results at a flagship price.

A single-dose-oriented grinder built around a 64mm flat hardened-steel burr set in a cast-aluminium grinding chamber with cooling ribs for heat dissipation. It uses a redesigned stepless grind adjustment allowing 0.1-step micro-adjustments, with an LC display visualising the grind size for repeatable settings. Retention is very low at about 0.2g or less, making it well suited to single dosing; the wide adjustment range covers espresso through filter brewing.

A compact portable manual hand grinder with a CNC-milled 38mm 440C stainless-steel conical burr, a machined anodised-aluminium housing and precision ball bearings, holding roughly 18g of beans. Grind size is set steplessly via a bottom adjustment nut from fine to coarse, with no marked scale. The stainless burr and ball-bearing-supported shaft give decent stability and durability for its class; marketed as a budget all-round grinder for home use and travel.

A budget hand grinder using 40mm ceramic conical burrs with an under-the-burr stepped adjustment ring offering roughly 18 click settings. Stainless steel body with a removable hand crank; ceramic burrs resist heat and wear but offer coarse, somewhat inconsistent grind uniformity. Intended as an entry-level manual grinder for drip, French press and casual brewing, with too much fines and bimodal distribution to satisfy serious espresso users. The wide stated range covers most brew methods but consistency degrades at the espresso-fine end.

A compact portable electric grinder built around 38mm stainless steel conical burrs with a USB-rechargeable motor and a stepped external selector covering roughly 40 click positions. Sleek minimalist anodized aluminum body designed for travel and on-the-go single-cup brewing. Grind quality is acceptable for filter, AeroPress and French press but lacks the fine-end consistency for true espresso. Marketed as a stylish all-in-one travel grinder prioritizing portability over ultimate uniformity.

A traditional Japanese-style manual mill using ceramic conical burrs with a stepless central adjustment nut beneath the hand crank for continuous grind control. Typically a glass or metal hopper and catch jar with a classic vintage aesthetic. The ceramic burrs are durable and corrosion-resistant, suited to filter, French press and general home brewing, though the open stepless mechanism makes precise repeatable settings harder. Best regarded as a decorative, casual-use grinder rather than a precision tool.

A compact electric home grinder from Kalita aimed at filter and pour-over brewing, with a small number of stepped adjustment positions (around 8). Plastic-bodied entry-level design intended for convenience and ease of use rather than precision. The limited range and coarse stepping mean it suits drip, French press and pour-over but not espresso. Grind consistency is modest, typical of low-cost electric box grinders.

A classic hand-crank mill from Kalita using ceramic conical burrs with a stepless adjustment nut for continuous grind sizing across a wide range. Features a retro design with wooden or metal accents and a drawer-style grounds catcher. Ceramic burrs provide durability and heat resistance, suited to filter, French press and everyday manual brewing. The stepless system offers flexibility but lacks precise repeatability, positioning it as a casual, aesthetic home grinder.

A premium Japanese electric grinder using ceramic flat ghost-burrs with a low-speed motor and built-in static-reducing and fines-removing design, adjusted via a dotted scale of around 11 stepped positions. Robust build with a die-cast body, known for low heat generation and clean, uniform grinds favored for pour-over and filter coffee. Includes a static-elimination mechanism and a chaff/fines separator for cleaner cups. Highly regarded among Japanese pour-over enthusiasts for consistency and low retention.

A respected Japanese electric grinder using cast-iron or steel flat-style cutter burrs with a low-speed gear motor for reduced heat and static, adjusted across roughly 6 stepped positions. Sturdy metal-bodied design prized for clean, consistent filter and pour-over grinds with minimal fines. Long popular in Japanese specialty cafes and homes for its reliability and grind clarity. Not suited to espresso, but excellent for drip, V60 and French press.

A minimalist hand grinder using 38mm stainless steel conical burrs housed in a clean cylindrical stainless body, with a numbered external rotation-based adjustment. Designed for portability and everyday filter, AeroPress and pour-over brewing with a focus on simple aesthetics. Grind consistency is good for the price in the medium-fine to medium range, though its capped range tops out below very coarse settings. Marketed as an approachable, well-built entry-to-mid hand grinder.

A compact 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click adjustment offering around 90 clicks for fine espresso-capable control. Aluminum body with a folding or detachable handle, positioned as the smallest, most travel-friendly model in the Kingrinder K-series. Delivers good uniformity for both espresso and filter at a budget price. The external adjustment makes dialing in fast and repeatable.

A 48mm stainless steel heptagonal conical-burr manual grinder featuring external numbered click adjustment with roughly 90 clicks for fine, repeatable control. Anodized aluminum body with a quality bearing-supported axle for stable, low-wobble grinding. Capable of espresso through French press with good consistency that punches above its price. The K1 is a popular value entry into precise external-adjust hand grinding.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click dial offering about 90 clicks for finer espresso resolution. Sturdy aluminum construction with dual bearings for axle stability and consistent particle distribution. Suited to espresso, pour-over and French press, it is a mid-tier K-series model valued for reliable dial-in repeatability. Build and grind quality are strong for the budget segment.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered adjustment of around 90 clicks, offering fine espresso-oriented stepping. Solid anodized aluminum body with twin bearings for a stable grind axle and even output. Versatile across espresso, AeroPress, pour-over and French press with consistency that competes with pricier grinders. A well-rounded value pick in the Kingrinder external-adjust lineup.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with a fine external numbered dial giving 80 clicks per rotation across multiple rotations (read as rotation.click, e.g. 2.42). Premium aluminum body with high-precision dual bearings for excellent axle stability and uniform grinding. The fine stepping suits precise espresso dial-in while the wide range also covers cold brew and French press. One of the more capable, espresso-focused models in the K-series.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click adjustment of about 90 clicks for repeatable fine control. Robust anodized aluminum housing with dual-bearing axle support for stable, consistent grinding. Handles espresso through coarse brew methods with strong uniformity for its class. A dependable mid-to-upper K-series external-adjust grinder offering good value.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with fine external numbered adjustment at 80 clicks per rotation (read as rotation.click) for precise espresso control. High-grade aluminum body with precision dual bearings delivering excellent grind stability and uniformity. The fine stepping and wide range make it strong for espresso while still covering filter, French press and cold brew. A flagship-level K-series grinder favored by espresso-focused home users.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder using an internal under-burr stepped adjustment rather than an external dial, with a wide range spanning espresso to cold brew. Aluminum body in the budget P-series, prioritizing simplicity and value over rapid external dial-in. Grind consistency is good for the price across filter, French press and espresso, though changing settings requires accessing the internal nut. Positioned as an affordable entry into Kingrinder hand grinders.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with internal stepped adjustment located beneath the burrs and a broad espresso-to-coarse range. Anodized aluminum construction with a detachable handle, sitting in the value-oriented internal-adjust P-series. Offers solid uniformity for filter, AeroPress, French press and espresso, with settings adjusted via the internal mechanism rather than a quick external dial. A capable budget hand grinder for users who set and forget their grind.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder featuring internal under-burr stepped adjustment and a wide range covering espresso through cold brew. Sturdy aluminum body with dual-bearing support for stable grinding, the upper model of the internal-adjust P-series. Delivers good grind consistency across espresso and filter methods at a competitive price. Best suited to users comfortable adjusting internally rather than needing fast external dial-in.

A premium German manual grinder with 47mm Black Fusion-coated stainless steel conical burrs and a heavy stainless steel/zinc-alloy body with auto-centering burr alignment. The stepless adjustment dial offers extremely fine resolution (around 5 microns per division) and a marked zero-point for repeatable settings, making it suitable for everything from Turkish and espresso through to coarse French press and cold brew. Built for durability and grind consistency, it is widely regarded as one of the best all-round manual grinders.

A lighter, more affordable version of the M47 sharing the same 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs and stepless adjustment, but with lightweight ABS components, a silicone grip band and an aluminium catch cup (around 780g). It omits the auto-centering technology and easy-hold thumb stopper of the Classic/Simplicity to cut cost and weight. Grind range and resolution match the rest of the M47 line, covering espresso to French press with fine, repeatable adjustment.

A streamlined M47 model with the same 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs and auto-centering system as the Classic, paired with a smooth stepless dial featuring a clearly marked zero-point for easy calibration. It weighs around 1050g with a 40g capacity and uses a magnetic catch cup and ABS thumb stopper. Offering adjustments as fine as roughly 5 microns, it handles all brew methods from espresso to cold brew with high consistency.

The lightest and most compact M47 designed for travel, retaining the 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs but in a packable, lightweight body. One division on its adjustment wheel changes grind size by about 10 microns, and because the system is stepless, adjustments as fine as 2 microns are possible. It covers the full brew spectrum from espresso to French press while prioritizing portability and durability.

An electric countertop burr grinder using stainless steel conical burrs driven by a 240W motor at around 450 RPM with a 200g bean hopper. It offers 15 stepped grind settings spanning a 280-1070 micron range, suited to drip, pour over and French press more than fine espresso. Housed in KitchenAid heavy die-cast metal Artisan body (around 4.5kg) for stability, it targets home users wanting a stylish, durable mid-range grinder.

An electric countertop grinder with commercial-grade stainless steel conical burrs and 70 stepped grind settings covering espresso to French press over a 240-1130 micron range. It features Smart Dosing Technology that auto-adjusts grind time for consistent output, plus anti-static technology to reduce mess, and a 280g bean hopper. Designed as a compact, cabinet-friendly all-rounder for home brewing, with solid grind consistency though it can be tricky to keep clean.

A compact, travel-oriented manual grinder built around 38mm titanium-nitride-coated steel conical burrs in a tough aluminium body, sized to fit inside an AeroPress for portability. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, with settings recorded as rotation, number and tick for repeatability, and a bottle-opener pointer indicating the position. It spans espresso to coarse brews and is prized for grind quality relative to its small size.
Verification Notes: Range from source (build wave); stepless so micron/step = NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: An updated version of the original Feldgrind featuring 38mm ceramic-coated steel conical burrs and a machined aluminium unibody design for improved alignment and durability. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, recorded as rotation and number for repeatable settings across espresso to French press. It has a longer, wider crank arm and larger wooden knob than the Aergrind for a smoother, more comfortable grind.
Verification Notes: Range from source (build wave); stepless so micron/step = NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A larger Knock manual grinder machined from a single piece of aerospace-grade aluminium for absolute burr concentricity, fitted with 47mm steel burrs that grind roughly 40% faster than the 38mm burrs in the smaller Felds and Aergrind. Its stepless, no-click dial offers very fine resolution of about 2 microns per degree of rotation across the full brewing spectrum. Settings are referenced as rotation, number and tick, making it a high-capacity, precise grinder for both espresso and filter.
Verification Notes: Range from source (build wave); stepless so micron/step = NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The original full-size Knock manual grinder with 38mm open stainless steel conical burrs in a machined aluminium body, featuring a longer wider crank arm and larger wooden knob than the Aergrind for smoother grinding. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, recorded as rotation and number for repeatable settings spanning espresso through coarse French press and cold brew. It is valued for solid build quality and versatile grind range in a portable hand grinder.
Verification Notes: Range from source (build wave); stepless so micron/step = NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: An electric flat-burr grinder using stainless steel burrs, with a front dial offering 9 grind-fineness settings and a separate selector for 2-12 cups. An entry-level domestic grinder aimed at drip and filter brewing, not espresso, with a 200g bean hopper. Build is largely plastic with a removable grind chamber, and grind consistency is modest with noticeable fines typical of budget burr grinders.

An electric flat stainless-steel burr grinder (Expert series) offering 17 grind settings spanning fine to coarse, plus a cup selector for 2-12 cups. It targets home users brewing drip, French press and pour over rather than true espresso. The housing is mostly plastic with a 200g hopper and removable grind container; grind uniformity is acceptable for the price but produces some fines at finer settings.

An electric flat-burr grinder (Expert series) with stainless steel burrs and an adjustment system marketed as 45 grind settings, combining 5 coarseness levels with 9 fineness positions. Designed for home brewing across espresso-to-French-press ranges, though best suited to drip and filter methods. It features a 200g hopper, mostly plastic construction and an anti-overflow safety system; consistency is reasonable for a domestic grinder.

An electric flat metal-burr grinder offering 45 grind settings via 5 coarseness and 9 fineness levels, with a digital display and timed dosing for 2-12 cups. A premium home grinder aimed at filter, drip and French press brewing rather than dedicated espresso. Construction mixes metal and plastic with a 250g hopper; some retention and fines are present but it delivers good versatility for everyday domestic use.

A commercial benchmark grinder built around 98mm flat steel burrs driven by a powerful direct-drive motor, prized for exceptional unimodal grind distribution and clarity in filter coffee. The (0-16) figure refers to the newer 0-16 numbered dial scale on the adjustment collar; it is the same machine as the 1-11 version, only the dial labeling differs. Adjustment is stepless via a continuously rotating collar, the housing is heavy cast metal, and it is intended for high-volume cafe and roastery use across all brew methods.

The same 98mm flat-burr EK43 commercial grinder, here referenced by the older 1-11 numbered dial scale on the adjustment collar rather than the newer 0-16 scale. It uses hardened steel flat burrs and a stepless adjustment collar for fine, continuous control, delivering the famously clean, uniform EK43 grind across espresso to French press. Construction is robust cast metal for heavy daily throughput; the 0-16 and 1-11 designations are merely different dial markings of an identical machine.

The more compact, cafe-oriented variant of the EK43, retaining the same 98mm flat steel burrs and stepless adjustment collar but in a smaller footprint with a shorter, espresso-friendly chute and bag-clip holder. It excels at both single-dosing espresso and filter work with the hallmark uniform, low-fines EK43 distribution. Build is heavy-duty cast metal for commercial reliability, and adjustment is continuous via a rotating dial measured in rotations from zero.

A heavy commercial/retail shop grinder built for grinding large volumes for whole-bean retail bagging, using large flat steel burrs and a powerful motor. Adjustment is via a collar marked in lines/numbers giving effectively stepless, fine control across a very wide range from Turkish-fine to coarse. It features a tall bulk hopper and rugged metal construction intended for supermarket and roastery counter use rather than home brewing.

A traditional commercial shop/retail grinder using 120mm flat steel burrs, with a doser and a labeled adjustment collar (Turkish, Fine, Cone, Basket, French Press, Percolator) offering stepless fine adjustment. It is built for high-volume cafe and retail grinding with a large bean hopper and heavy cast-metal body, and supports SSP 120mm burr upgrades for improved grind quality. Intended for traditional espresso and filter service rather than single-dosing.
Verification Notes: Stepless collar (Mahlkonig) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: The white-housing variant of the VTA 6S commercial shop grinder, sharing the same 120mm flat steel burrs, doser and stepless labeled adjustment collar (Turkish through Percolator). Designed for high-throughput cafe and retail use with a large hopper and durable cast-metal construction, and accepts the same SSP 120mm burr upgrades for finer grind tuning. Grind behavior is identical to the standard VTA 6S, differing only in colorway.
Verification Notes: Stepless collar (Mahlkonig) per official - micron/step NA

Grinder Characteristics & Coffee Brewing: A compact home all-rounder grinder built around 54mm flat steel burrs, with a stepless, continuously adjustable dial (numbered roughly 1-35) that handles espresso through French press. It single-doses or uses a small hopper, has a relatively low-retention design with a knocker, and a mostly plastic-and-metal domestic build. It delivers solid grind uniformity for a home grinder, bridging the gap between budget grinders and Mahlkonig commercial models.

A 45mm self-developed 7-axis Star-Tooth stainless steel conical-style burr (37.5mm inner / 45mm outer) with a 160-spike pre-breaker core and an aggressive hatch-cut lower section resembling a ghost burr. CNC aluminum body with external numbered click adjustment (0.0167mm per click). Mid-range manual grinder aimed at all-round use from espresso to pour-over, praised for clarity and an unusually large burr for its price class.

83mm flat steel burrs driven by a belt-driven 900W motor at ~900 RPM with a double-fan cooling system. Electronic stepper-motor grind adjustment measures true burr gap in microns with TRUE ZERO calibration for repeatability across units. Single-dose capable with sub-0.45g retention; a professional/prosumer grinder for espresso and filter, upgradable with SSP 83mm burrs.

83mm special-steel flat burrs with digital micron-accurate electronic grind adjustment and TRUE ZERO cross-unit calibration. Low-RPM motor with double-fan cooling and a side-flipping digital display for chamber access. Multifunction single-dose-capable commercial grinder with portafilter holder, filter canister and bag holder for both espresso and filter, upgradable with SSP burrs.

Steel conical-burr home grinder with 39 grind settings and an integrated scale offering three dosing modes (cups, weight to the gram, and manual/time). 375g bean hopper and removable grounds container with dishwasher-safe parts. Aimed at everyday filter and cafetiere users (espresso not really in range), valued for accurate weight-based dosing rather than fine espresso resolution.

Stainless steel flat-burr entry-level electric grinder with 17 fineness settings from espresso (1-3) through filter (4-12) to French press (13-17). 200g bean container, grinds enough for 2-14 cups per cycle, with automatic shut-off to prevent overheating. A budget household grinder best suited to filter and press brewing rather than precise espresso.

A 48mm 7-core CNC420 stainless steel conical burr with 45-degree cutting edges and HRC55-60 hardness for fast, durable cutting. External numbered click dial moves the burr 16 microns per click across 180 total clicks. A premium manual grinder with external adjustment aimed at both espresso and pour-over, sold with sifting screens in the Pro kit.

An entry-level single-dose electric grinder with 7-core 40mm stainless steel (420) conical burrs and external adjustment (60 clicks per rotation, ~80 soft clicks) over a stepless base. 150W motor, ~40g hopper, sub-3kg footprint, includes a 58mm magnetic dosing cup. Rebadged as the Turin SD40; positioned as a budget single-dose grinder for espresso, filter and French press.

50mm flat steel burrs with a stepless adjustment dial marked 1 (fine) to 9 (coarse), driven by a quiet direct-drive motor that grinds 60g in under 30 seconds. ECBC-certified with a recommended filter setting of 5. A filter-focused home grinder designed to pair with Moccamaster brewers; not intended for espresso.

Compact portable manual grinder with 38mm 420 stainless steel hexagonal conical burrs (55-58 HRC) for uniformity and reduced fines. 6063/6061 aluminum-alloy body, ~30g capacity, ~430g weight, fully disassemblable including the burrs. 36-level click adjustment counted by clicks; a budget travel grinder for espresso through French press.

Hardened alloy steel 40mm conical-burr electric grinder with 16 grind settings and a gear-reduction motor that grinds slowly for reduced noise and heat. Detachable PowderBlock grinding chamber with an embedded cleaning brush, one-touch operation with auto-stop, and a large hopper/grounds capacity. A budget household grinder oriented to drip and press brewing rather than fine espresso.

63mm hardened steel conical burrs (Mazzer Kony type) with an infinite stepless adjustment dial and a near-zero-retention direct grind path for single dosing. Aluminum body with real oak trim, ~30g bean cup, grinds at 1-2g/sec at low RPM for low heat, with a safety-interlock lid. A popular prosumer single-dose grinder spanning espresso to French press.

A compact electric grinder with a choice of 48mm or 65mm in-house Mizen conical burrs and stepless adjustment marked for easy dialing in. CNC-machined aluminum components to sub-10-micron tolerances, a brushless DC motor with planetary gearset, and a built-in clicker giving dynamic retention under 0.1g for single dosing. A versatile single-dose home grinder from espresso to cold brew.

An ultra-compact single-dose electric grinder using 48mm in-house Moonshine conical burrs, with stepless adjustment via the threaded single-dose collar tensioned by three internal springs (10 large dots plus 4 minor dots between each). Aerospace-grade aluminum body throughout. A small-footprint multipurpose grinder aimed at espresso and filter for compact setups.

The same ultra-compact single-dose Lagom Mini platform fitted with the original 38mm Obsidian conical burrs (since discontinued in favor of Moonshine).
